response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with 15+ years of experience, but his background is overwhelmingly web and full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than embedded networking. He fails a hard requirement outright: the role demands native-level Japanese, and he lists only English, Romanian, and basic German. His C experience is brief and dated (a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), and there is no evidence of Linux kernel source modification, L2/L3 switch development, or any of the security technologies central to this role (MACsec, 802.1X, tamper detection).
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput microservices) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a non-negotiable requirement for the role
No embedded networking or security domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, switch configuration, kernel hardening)
C for UNIX experience is minimal and roughly a decade old, with no Linux kernel modification track record

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Long, versatile senior engineering track record
